event_blocks.summary: The segment begins with the distinct sound of paper rustling and a 'pop' as a man in a dark suit reads a 'TASKMASTER' envelope at a dimly lit desk, transitioning into a montage with upbeat music. This montage features a man in a red tracksuit shoveling snow outside a brick house, sitting in a white tent with a digital timer at '0.0' and messy food, joyfully running through a shopping mall, and a close-up of a yellow rubber duck in a bathtub with a 'Melon buffet' note. Following more paper rustling, a 'thud,' and a 'whoosh,' the scene shifts to a theatrical stage where the host, Greg Davies, in a black suit, stands on an ornate red and gold stage holding the 'TASKMASTER' envelope. He introduces himself and the show to a panel of four, explaining his role is to make "accomplished but needy comedians do stupid things for me. Why? Because I can. I'm a powerful man. Look at me. Thank you." The panel reacts with smiles and raised eyebrows as audience laughter is heard. Greg Davies states the contestants "have no idea how each other did, but they will soon find out," and that he will be "judging both what they did and how they did it. My word is final. The contestants will definitely respect me." He then begins introducing the first contestant. The video then shows a close-up of the host, Greg Davies, smiling warmly on stage, followed by a wide shot of the theatrical stage with red and gold decor. Greg Davies, holding the 'TASKMASTER' envelope, introduces the panel members: Frank Skinner, Josh Widdicombe (in a light blue shirt), Roisin Conaty (woman with curly blonde hair), and Romesh Ranganathan (man with a beard), each introduction met with applause. He then sits in a golden chair and speaks with his co-host, Alex Horne, who is seated beside him with a tablet-like device. Greg asks Alex if they should have banter, to which Alex agrees. The host and co-host are seated in ornate golden thrones on stage. Alex explains the 'prize task,' where contestants brought personal possessions for the prize hall, to be scored based on how impressed the hosts are, with the host scoring based on the 'unusualness' of the item. Close-ups of the panel members, including Frank Skinner, Josh, a woman in a patterned dress, and another man in a dark suit, show their reactions. A large screen displays an image of sparkly scissors with the 'TASKMASTER' logo. Frank Skinner humorously reveals he brought 'grape scissors' with grapes on the shaft, joking they looked like 'hemorrhoid tweezers,' eliciting laughter. Greg questions the unusualness of grape cutters and turns to Josh. The man in the light blue shirt comments on a 'football signed by the Plymouth Argyle...' The host discusses a 'squad that lost in the Division 2 1994 playoff semi-finals,' humorously asking, 'Did they use invisible ink?' The man in the light blue shirt notes, 'I don't know what's happened to the signature,' which the host deems 'unusual.' The man with a beard and glasses adds, 'It's the most unusual signed football I've ever seen.' The woman with curly blonde hair describes finding an 'old tramp poet' who 'carves on benches' and taking a photo of him next to a bench he carved, which causes laughter. A framed image shows a man in a black beanie on a bench with text about a 'tramp poet' and a red lifebuoy. The co-host recounts paying for lunch and petrol on a trip, sparking laughter, and the woman with curly blonde hair interjects that he didn't pay for lunch. Greg jokes, 'You let a tramp pay for your lunch!' leading to loud laughter. Greg declares, 'I was going to put you in last place, but Romesh has just snatched that from you,' before asking Romesh what he brought in.

event_blocks.summary: On the ornate red and gold 'Taskmaster' stage, a large screen displayed a festive snow globe image with three children photoshopped as Santas, which the Man in black suit, seated in a golden throne, described as the 'best Christmas present in the history of Christmas presents,' drawing laughter from the audience and the four panel members. A close-up highlighted the snow globe's details, including the children's faces and its snowflake-patterned base. The host critiqued its 'poor' snow density, noting it only flowed for 'two seconds maximum,' which he deemed 'awful.' Tim Key, the Man with beard and glasses, inquired if it was signed, and the host confirmed all three children had signed it. The Man in light blue shirt humorously commented on the host's 'fury' over the snow globe, as the host defended his passion. The host then asked Tim Key what he brought, leading to a screen transition showing a mounted deer skull with antlers, which Tim Key identified as a 'Reindeer skull,' prompting further audience laughter and panel reactions before the segment concluded.

event_blocks.summary: The show opens with a stylized title sequence featuring a vintage typewriter typing 'TASKMASTER' above five contestant portraits, transitioning to a stage where the Host, a man in a black suit, seated in a golden chair, announces the scoreboard, placing Josh Widdicombe last and Tim Key first, met with audience applause. The Host introduces the next challenge, set in his lab at his Taskmaster retreat, transitioning to an exterior of a brick house and then a vintage alarm clock showing 1:05. Inside a room with a wooden table, a white protective suit, blue goggles, and a sealed envelope are laid out. The Man in the light blue shirt enters, dons the suit and goggles, remarking on the 'kinky' feel and resemblance to school science lessons, before sitting at the table to open the sealed envelope. The Woman in pink lace top and the Man in red tracksuit also don their suits and goggles as the Host's voiceover instructs them to eat as much watermelon as possible within one minute, starting when they open the door to the lab. Josh, in a white protective suit and blue goggles, stands at a wooden table, holding a 'TASKMASTER' envelope, expressing frustration about having already eaten a watermelon. On stage, host Greg Davies, in a black suit, sits in a golden throne, expressing his love for watermelon but anticipating it being ruined by the task, explaining five comedians are forced to eat as much watermelon as possible while wearing white boiler suits. The panel, including a man in a light blue shirt and a man with a beard and glasses, react with amusement. Greg asks Josh if he's a melon fan, to which Josh replies, 'Fine, seven out of ten.' Greg then shows Josh's attempt: to eat as much watermelon as possible in the time allowed. Josh, in a sterile white tent, stands at a white table with a watermelon and utensils, concerned about needing a knife. He begins cutting and scooping the watermelon flesh into a glass bottle, with prominent cutting and scooping sounds. The host remarks on the effort, and the co-host responds dryly. A panel member with a beard and glasses critiques the pace, comparing it to his grandmother eating melon, while the man in the light blue shirt playfully interjects. The co-host jokingly accuses Josh of slowing down the footage. The man with the beard and glasses reveals Josh ate 218 grams of watermelon in one minute, equivalent to 10 mice, which the host deems 'pretty good.'

raw_segments: The video opens with a stylized title sequence featuring a vintage typewriter typing 'TASKMASTER' above five golden-framed contestant portraits, each with a red wax seal. The scene transitions to a stage where the Host, a man with short gray hair in a black suit, seated in an ornate golden chair, announces the scoreboard, stating, "score board looks like this. Josh Widdicombe in last place, Tim Key in first place." This is met with enthusiastic applause from the audience and the four Panel members, who react with amusement and attentiveness. Beside the Host, the Co-host, a man with short brown hair and a beard, listens while holding a black tablet-like device. The Host then introduces the next challenge, stating, "On with the next task, the first task proper. This took place in my lab at my Taskmaster retreat. And for good reason." An upbeat, quirky instrumental track begins as the video transitions to an exterior shot of a brick house with a red door, then a close-up of a vintage alarm clock showing 1:05. The scene moves inside to a room with a wooden table, where a white protective suit, blue goggles, and a sealed envelope are laid out. The Man in light blue shirt, who is a panel member, enters the room, saying, "I'll put this on first though." As he dons the white protective suit and blue goggles, he chuckles and remarks, "I'll be honest, this feels a bit kinky." He continues to suit up, adding, "Used to be a guy in my village, used to wear one of these," accompanied by the rustling sounds of the protective suit. Fully suited, he exclaims with a laugh, "Is this my task?" He then muses, "God, I feel like I'm back at school in science lessons," and adds, "Ah, I really, I really need my reading goggles." Meanwhile, the Woman in pink lace top and the Man in red tracksuit also enter the room, each donning their white protective suits and blue goggles, as the Host's voiceover instructs, "In the lab there is a watermelon. Eat as much watermelon as possible." The Man in the light blue shirt, now fully suited, sits at the wooden table, opens the sealed envelope, and reads its contents with a focused expression. The Woman in the pink top and the Man in red tracksuit follow suit, all appearing ready for the challenge, as the Host's voice concludes the instructions: "You have one minute. Your time starts when you open the door to the lab."

The video opens with Josh, a man in a white protective suit and blue goggles, standing at a wooden table in a brightly lit room with pop-art portraits. He holds a 'TASKMASTER' envelope with a red wax seal, preparing for a task, and states in a slightly frustrated tone, "Frustrating because I just had one [watermelon] in my dressing room." The setting then shifts to a grand theater stage with ornate red and gold decor, where host Greg Davies, a man in a black suit, sits in a golden throne-like chair, holding the same 'TASKMASTER' envelope. He explains the show's tone, noting, "there's five comedians being forced to eat as much watermelon as they can, whilst wearing a white boiler suit." Beside him, the co-host in a golden chair holds a black tablet-like device. The panel of four, including a man in a light blue shirt and a man with a beard and glasses, react with amusement and attentiveness, with laughter heard from the audience. The co-host, a man with a beard in a black suit seated in a golden throne and holding a black device, answers concisely, "54 seconds." The **Man in dark suit with tie** exclaims, "Oh dear, oh dear! Son of a..." followed by laughter, while the **Man in light blue shirt** declares, "I love melon." The host continues, questioning, "How could you possibly at the end of that task, go, 'Do you know what I fancy?'" and explains that Tim "gobbled an extra bit of melon," which would make him the winner if counted, but clarifies, "I didn't weigh that bit because it was in Tim." The segment ends with close-ups of the **Man in dark suit with tie** and the **Man with beard and glasses** displaying amusement and contemplation, as the host holds the envelope and speaks with authority, discussing subtracting "nine from..."

raw_segments: The segment opens with a close-up of the Man with beard and glasses, who appears contemplative against a dark red backdrop, as the Man in black suit, the host, states, "it would put Ramesh in the lead. That's up to you, Ramesh." The scene shifts to the host, seated in an ornate golden throne, holding a 'TASKMASTER' envelope, speaking and gesturing to the panel, questioning, "Did he wink at us?" Beside him, the Co-host in golden chair, identified as Alex, responds, "it's very hard to tell. If it was a wink, it was a bad wink," adding, "but um, we're gonna have a look." The camera pans to the Panel members – including the Man in dark suit with tie, the Man in light blue shirt, the Woman with curly blonde hair, and the Man in dark suit with black tie – who react with amusement, accompanied by audience laughter. The host continues, declaring, "If that's a wink, that's that's such breathtaking arrogance from Tim Key," and asks the audience to decide if it was a wink, prompting a loud cheer. He then announces, "The winner is Ramesh Ranganathan!" to enthusiastic audience applause and cheers, adding, "Good work, Ramesh. One step closer to the reindeer skull." A graphic overlay appears, showing a vintage typewriter with framed portraits of the panel members, each with a score. The host then asks, "Alex, how are the scores looking?" and Alex, the Co-host, details, "the leader is Tim Key, followed by Ramesh, and Rosheen and Josh are in last and second last," which elicits an audience groan followed by more applause. The Woman with curly blonde hair speaks animatedly, followed by the Man in dark suit with black tie gesturing. event_blocks.summary: The Taskmaster, a man in a black suit, seated in an ornate golden throne, announced a live task involving "ornate grape cutting scissors," prompting audience laughter, before asking his co-host, Alex, also in a black suit, "What's next, Alex?" Alex responded, "We have some extreme art," as a large screen displayed sparkling scissors and a whistle sound was heard, followed by upbeat, slightly comedic instrumental music. The scene shifted to a farm where a white envelope with a red wax seal lay in hay next to a horse in a stable. A woman in a black jacket over a pink top picked up the envelope and approached a man in a dark jacket holding a similar envelope. A man in a red tracksuit, now wearing a white headband, put on a black helmet and smiled, while the man in the dark jacket adjusted his black cap. The woman in the pink top looked surprised holding her envelope. Back on stage, the panel members—a man in a dark suit with a tie, a man in a light blue shirt, a woman with curly blonde hair, and a man in a dark suit with a black tie—reacted with amusement. A wide shot showed the host and co-host in their thrones, the panel, and the audience, with a close-up of the envelope on screen. The panel discussed the task: the man in the dark jacket questioned, "Is this necessary, is it?" The woman in the pink lace top exclaimed "Wow" and groaned, while the man in the red tracksuit stated, "Paint a horse." The man in the dark jacket clarified, "Paint the best picture of a horse," and the man in the light blue shirt suggested, "Boss riding a horse." The man in the dark jacket admitted, "I've never ridden a horse before," and the man in the dark suit with a tie added, "And I am absolutely dog at painting." The man in the red tracksuit felt more comfortable painting a horse than riding one. The man in the dark suit with a tie summarized his predicament, "Can't paint, can't ride horses," while the man in the light blue shirt confidently stated, "I can draw a horse." The woman in the pink lace top playfully asked if she'd get points for "riding a horse in my mind." The upbeat music faded back in as the segment concluded. The Man in black suit, Greg Davies, holding a 'TASKMASTER' envelope, and the Co-host in golden chair, Alex Horne, holding a tablet, were seated in ornate golden thrones. Greg explained the challenge was "to capture the essence of horse," before the camera revealed the full theatrical stage with Panel members. Alex confirmed a framed photograph of a brown and white horse named 'BAZ' was "Baz," prompting Greg to remark, "not a bad horse." Greg instructed Alex to judge purely on gut reactions. The scene transitioned to a wall displaying five framed abstract horse paintings, described by Alex as "painted whilst riding horses," eliciting audience laughter. Greg confidently stated judging would be "pretty straightforward." The Man in light blue shirt smiled, the Man with beard and glasses laughed heartily, and the Woman with curly blonde hair expressed surprise. Greg declared the bottom-right painting "clearly the best picture of the horse," to which the Woman with curly blonde hair shouted "No!" causing audience laughter and applause. The bottom-right painting was awarded a blue ribbon with the number '2'. Greg described the next horse as an "impressionist version" with "some degree of artistic flair," which the audience affirmed with a "Yeah" and applause, confirming it as "second." Greg began to describe the top three as "absolutely..." before a bell chime and audience laughter concluded the segment, showing the top three paintings, including a yellow horse with blue and red accents, as the panel reacted with amusement. Greg Davies animatedly described a painting on the right with a bridle, stating, "that person probably can dress themselves," eliciting audience laughter. He continued, "The next one, incredibly bad," as the camera cut to a wide shot of the Panel members reacting with laughter and attentiveness. Greg Davies pointed out a painting "so dramatically overshadowed by a picture of a giant mouse," drawing more laughter and applause. As framed abstract horse paintings with blue ribbons labeled '3', '4', and '5' were shown, he exclaimed, "Genuinely don't know who painted these, but that is one of the worst paintings I've ever seen in my life. In fact, it's genuinely infuriating me," prompting further audience amusement. Alex Horne announced a pause for a break. Upbeat, quirky theme music played with sound effects and audience applause. Greg Davies welcomed back the audience, asking, "Where were we?" Alex Horne asked if they'd like to find out who painted the paintings, to which the audience responded "Yes!" Greg Davies, still referring to the "mouse," was corrected by the Man in dark suit with black tie, Romesh Ranganathan, who interjected, "It's a horse, mate," drawing more laughter. Greg Davies conceded, "Oh, I now I think I have a better idea," before beginning to reveal the artists' names, stating, "Okay, so here are the names of the artists. So yeah, Tim Key painted the worst one. Frank Skinner painted the..." The scene concluded with a wide view of the stage, showing a large screen displaying five framed abstract horse paintings, each with a blue ribbon and nameplates: 'TIM', 'JOSH', 'ROISIN', 'ROMESH', and 'FRANK', as Greg Davies gestured and Alex Horne laughed.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a man, later identified as Frank, in a black helmet peeking through the slats of a wooden stable door, accompanied by his grunting and straining sounds as he attempts a task. The scene quickly shifts to a large indoor riding arena with wooden walls and sand flooring, where Frank, in a dark jacket and helmet, rides a dapple-gray horse while holding a white canvas and a paintbrush. He vocalizes his struggle, stating, "Just difficult to express myself artistically in this, in these conditions." Another panel member's voice asks, "How do you make brown?", to which Frank replies, "How do you think? Blue?" Laughter from the panel and audience is heard. Frank then expresses satisfaction, "I'm quite pleased with this," as he continues his task. Another panel member comments, "Who says men can't multitask?" Frank is followed by a man in a bright red tracksuit with white stripes, who also rides the same horse, energetically painting on a canvas while galloping, though no specific audio is attributed to him. Frank dismounts and hands the canvas to the man in the red tracksuit, declaring, "Okay, I've done. I've definitely done. It's not gonna get better," and reiterating, "I'm quite pleased with that." The scene cuts to a theatrical stage with ornate golden thrones, where a panel of judges, including the host (a man in a black suit holding a 'TASKMASTER' envelope) and a co-host with a tablet, observe the footage on a large screen. The judges react with amusement and commentary, followed by a burst of audience applause. The host then speaks, noting a contestant's "grossly conspicuous" absence, implying they either did "incredibly well or incredibly badly." He then addresses Frank directly, "Frank, do you paint? Your painting's lovely, aren't you?" Frank responds, revealing, "You know what? I tell you, a weird thing has happened. I've never painted before since school." The video then shows a framed image of Frank with a blue ribbon labeled '1' and his name 'FRANK' beneath it, visually confirming his identity and a potential first-place ranking, followed by a close-up of the host smiling, highlighting the evaluative and comedic atmosphere of the show.

event_blocks.summary: On a theatrical stage adorned with red patterned wallpaper, chandeliers, and a 'TM' logo, five panel members in golden chairs and the Man in black suit, the host, seated in a golden throne, engage in a lively discussion. The host, holding a 'TASKMASTER' envelope, humorously states, "I might start painting... I'm less good not on a horse," prompting audience and panel laughter. He then questions the Man with beard and glasses, Josh Widdicombe, about his parents' horses, to which Josh declares, "I hate horses," and clarifies his Devon origins. Romesh Ranganathan, the Man in dark suit with tie, humorously counters, "No, they live in a council flat in Ealing," drawing significant audience laughter and applause. The camera focuses on a framed abstract horse painting labeled 'ROMESH' with a blue ribbon marked '2', as the host remarks on Romesh's apparent happiness. Romesh explains his artistic immersion, comparing it to Neo in "The Matrix" seeing "everything in code" and feeling he "saw Baz's spirit." The host interrupts with a bleeped expletive, followed by more laughter. The host then asks the Man in dark suit with black tie, Tim Key, if he knows how to make bran, receiving a simple "No." The energetic, humorous atmosphere is maintained by the host and co-host, with expressive contributions from the panelists under warm theatrical lighting.

event_blocks.summary: The Man in black suit, seated on a golden throne, addresses the Panel members, referencing a previous task and a score of 'blue,' prompting laughter. Positioned in front of a large mirror in a bright, modern room with white walls and a large window showing a pastoral scene, she begins painting on a canvas held before her as the mechanical horse moves. Roisin's voice is heard stating, "I don't like fan B," and later describing the mechanical horse as "a kind of way bit better than I thought, it's a bit like my little pony," which is followed by audience applause and cheers. event_blocks.summary: The Man in black suit, Greg Davies, disqualified the Woman with curly blonde hair, Roisin, from a task for not painting a horse while riding one, placing her last, which prompted her vocal protest about disqualification versus last place. The Man in dark suit with black tie, Josh, offered to go third, eliciting audience laughter. Greg Davies confirmed Roisin's last place for the infraction and declared the Man in dark suit with tie, Frank Skinner, the winner, who audibly confirmed the decision as fair. A wide shot showed the four Panel members reacting with surprise and laughter. Greg Davies then asked the Co-host in golden chair, Alex Horne, for the scores; Alex reported a tight race with Romesh in the lead and Tim and Frank in second, as graphics of framed abstract horse paintings and a vintage typewriter displayed the updated scores. Greg Davies then announced the next task, transitioning to an exterior shot of a brick house, a vintage alarm clock showing 1:05, and a yellow rubber duck, accompanied by upbeat, quirky instrumental music. Playful background music accompanied a scene where a man in a dark jacket and white headband dramatically dived into an outdoor white clawfoot bathtub containing a yellow rubber duck, causing a splash and sending the duck flying before he emerged holding a sealed white envelope. The woman with curly blonde hair also received a similar envelope with excitement. On stage, Greg Davies and Alex Horne held their envelopes as Greg Davies instructed, "Completely empty this bathtub. Fastest wins. You must not remove the plug, tip or damage the bath. The time starts now." The woman contestant reacted with surprise, and Greg Davies clarified the task, mentioning he had grouped Frank and Josh together. Josh, the Man with curly light brown hair, approached the outdoor bathtub on a raised platform with yellow lighting stands, pouring water from a metal jug into it and musing about the constraints. He questioned the method, asking if they had a Hoover, as a digital timer counted down. Later, the Man with curly light brown hair was seen pouring water from a metal jug into the soapy bathtub, contemplating siphoning methods. He then decided to use a blue garden hose, spraying water into the tub, creating a large splash. He then grabbed a black trash can, inverted it, and plunged it into the tub, causing a massive splash, before carrying the water-filled can away, smiling. The yellow rubber duck remained floating in the foam amidst the brick house and a small shed.

The video opens with a close-up of a white clawfoot bathtub outdoors, filled with soapy water and a bright yellow rubber duck floating inside. A man, identified as the 'Man with curly light brown hair', wearing a dark blue shirt and black trousers, is seen pouring water from a metal jug into the tub, causing the duck to bob amidst the sounds of water splashing. He muses about siphoning, stating, "I mean that's got death written all over it, hasn't it?" and recalling how "ruffians used to do with petrol tanks" or "soapy water," adding, "It's not how I remember it." The scene then widens to reveal the bathtub on a raised platform in a grassy yard in front of a two-story brick house, illuminated by two tall yellow lighting stands. The man walks toward the tub carrying a blue garden hose, smiling, and after contemplating, "I'm tempted to just go for it with a bucket," he decides, "No, I'm gonna go for it with a bucket." He connects the hose and begins spraying water into the tub, creating a large splash that sends foam and water flying, accompanied by intensifying water sounds and an upbeat, whimsical background music that begins around 0:45. He laughs loudly, exclaiming "Oh!" and "Woo! This is so tough!" as he leans over the tub, adjusting the hose. Suddenly, he grabs a large black trash can, tips it upside down, and plunges it into the tub, causing a massive splash that erupts outward, drenching the surrounding area with loud water sounds. He then lifts the can, now full of soapy water, and carries it away, laughing. The scene ends with him placing the can on the ground and walking back toward the tub, still smiling, as the yellow rubber duck remains floating amidst the foam, with the brick house and a small shed visible in the background.

event_blocks.summary: The Man with curly light brown hair, clad in a dark jacket and light blue scarf, energetically fills a white clawfoot bathtub outdoors before a brick house, using a metal jug, a blue garden hose, and a black trash can amidst upbeat, chaotic music, laughing and grunting as he creates a soapy mess under two yellow lighting stands. He questions the need for dryness and laments the difficulty of removing the last "dregs," concluding, "There's some bits, but I think that's my best go." He then dives into the tub, retrieves a sealed envelope, and emerges surprised. raw_segments: The minute opens with a wide shot of a brick house and a white clawfoot bathtub on a raised platform, accompanied by light, whimsical background music. A male voice (likely the Taskmaster) states "One minute," to which she responds, "I've got one minute," before he teases, "No, one minute's gone," eliciting an "Oh" from her. She then begins filling the tub with water using a metal jug, a blue garden hose, and a large black trash can, creating dramatic splashes and foam, with constant sounds of water pouring, sloshing, and gurgling. Another male voice (likely Alex Horne) advises, "Might want to move back, guys." The Woman laughs and declares, "I've got a system, I'm sticking with it," as she continues her chaotic efforts, occasionally grunting. She mutters, "Maybe over the side," and later states, "I've done about half," sounding pleased. After more intense splashing, she humorously remarks, "Made a big lunch, didn't I?" and then, with a pained laugh, "Oh my god. That is really hurt my back." She observes, "It's very deep," before concluding, "I think that's it. It's a bit left, I don't think I'm going to get it out." She then dives into the now-full, foamy tub, retrieves a sealed envelope, and emerges smiling. The scene cuts to a close-up of a man with short brown hair and a beard observing, then transitions to a studio setting. Here, the Woman with curly blonde hair is seated on stage with other panel members, including a man in a dark suit and a man in a light blue shirt. The studio audience's laughter is heard as the male voice (Alex Horne) asks, "How would you get that in bit out?" followed by loud applause and cheering, highlighting the contrast between the physical challenge and the panel’s amused commentary.

event_blocks.summary: On a theatrical stage bathed in warm, dramatic lighting, the Man in black suit, acting as host, held a 'TASKMASTER' envelope sealed with red wax and discussed a previous round's 'wink,' proposing a two-place demotion. The camera shifted to the panel members—the Man in dark suit with tie, the Man in light blue shirt, the Woman in pink lace top, and the Man with beard and glasses (Tim Key)—seated on red chairs. The Man in dark suit with tie concurred with the host, while the Woman in pink lace top exclaimed, 'He took the plug out!' The Man in light blue shirt echoed this sentiment, shouting, 'Shut up, Rosh! He took the plug out!' to audience laughter. Tim Key requested a one-place demotion, but the host firmly denied it, insisting that removing the bath plug merited last place. Tim Key's accusation of the host being 'judgmental' elicited audience laughter, a buzzer sound, and applause. The Woman in pink lace top protested, 'I went last place!' which the Man in light blue shirt confirmed. The Man in dark suit with tie argued for the other contestant to honorably accept last place for cheating. The host finalized his decision, placing the contestant last and stating, 'Judgment, done,' to applause. He then announced Romesh as the task winner, drawing cheers and applause, and remarked to Tim Key, 'We're learning a lot about you today, Tim Key.' The segment ended with the host announcing a break before the next task, captured in a wide shot of the stage, panel, and audience.

event_blocks.summary: On a theatrical stage adorned with ornate red and gold decor and a prominent 'TM' logo, four panel members, each holding an olive-green pop-up tent with a red 'TM' emblem, stand ready. Amidst audience laughter and sounds, a male panel member questions the tent placement, to which the Taskmaster dismissively replies, "All that, you can do internally." event_blocks.summary: On a theatrical stage adorned with ornate red and gold backdrops and a large 'TM' logo, four panel members emerge from behind olive-green pop-up tents, each bearing a red 'TM' emblem. The Man in black suit (Host), seated in a golden throne, humorously comments on their participation, asking, "You have to get in it! You're not in it!" and "She's not in the tent!" as the Woman in pink lace top laughs audibly. The Host inquires about "Frank" being in a tent for hours, building audience anticipation with cheers.
